rationale: Title X is more precise and informative, specifying the exact anatomical/technical challenge ('jailed transvalvular lead') rather than the broader, vaguer 'cardiac implantable electronic device leads,' and its colon-subtitle structure is tighter JACC style.
loser_weakness: Title Y uses generic terminology ('cardiac implantable electronic device leads') that dilutes specificity, and the prepositional phrase 'in Patients With' adds length without adding clarity compared to X's direct noun-phrase construction.
